steps.title: Mark the expected change
steps.content: The first sentence explains that ticker-tape originally came from telegraph tickers. By the 2008 Giants parade, those machines had long since been retired, so the sentence needs a transition that presents this as expected background.

steps.title: Reject example and result links
steps.content: A would make the 2008 sentence an example of ticker use, but it says tickers were gone. B merely adds information without signaling expectation. D wrongly makes retirement a result of earlier parade practices. The transition should mean "naturally" or "unsurprisingly." The answer is C.
